text.title: Spec Requirements Examples
text.summary: Concrete FR, NFR, AC, user story, and persona examples with common mistakes and fixes.

example.note: Persona: Freelance Developer "Alex". Role: Solo developer building SaaS products. Goal: Ship MVPs in weeks without managing auth/payments/infra. Pain Points: Too much time on boilerplate. Context: Works from home on macOS, deploys to Vercel, uses GitHub daily.

antipattern.description: Persona: "Power User" — goal: "use the product effectively".
antipattern.reason: No role, no pain points, no context. Cannot guide any design decision.

example.note: US-001: As a freelance developer (Alex), I want to create an account with my email, so that I can save API keys and project settings between sessions. Priority: Must (MVP). INVEST: Independent — no other story required; Testable — AC-001 exists.

antipattern.description: As a user, I want to log in, so that I can use the site.
antipattern.reason: No persona specificity, no measurable benefit clause. Cannot validate INVEST criteria.

example.note: FR-001: System SHALL allow users to register with email and password. Email SHALL match RFC 5321 format. Password SHALL be minimum 8 characters with at least 1 uppercase letter and 1 digit. Traces to: US-001. AC: AC-001, AC-002. Priority: Must.

antipattern.description: FR-001: System should validate user input properly.
antipattern.reason: "Should" is not mandatory, "properly" is ambiguous, no validation rules, no trace, no AC reference.

example.note: NFR-001: Login response time SHALL be less than 500ms at p95, measured server-side (excluding network latency) under a load of 1000 concurrent users. Validation: load test with k6. Priority: Must.

antipattern.description: NFR-001: System should be fast and responsive.
antipattern.reason: No quantified target, no measurement conditions, not testable.

example.note: AC-001 (Happy Path): Given user is on /register, And email "test@example.com" is not in the system, When user enters email "test@example.com" and password "SecurePass1!", And clicks Register, Then account is created in the database, And user receives a verification email within 30 seconds, And user is redirected to /dashboard.

AC-002 (Error): Given user is on /register, When user submits password "weak", Then a validation error "Password must be at least 8 characters with 1 uppercase and 1 digit" is displayed inline, And no account is created.

antipattern.description: AC-001: When user submits valid data, then the system responds correctly.
antipattern.reason: "Valid data" and "responds correctly" are placeholders. Cannot be executed as an automated test.

example.note: Out of Scope: Social login (Google, GitHub) — Phase 2, post-MVP. Reason: OAuth provider complexity. Two-factor authentication — Phase 3. Reason: Added complexity vs. MVP value. SSO/SAML — Not planned. Reason: Enterprise-only requirement, not current market.

antipattern.reason: Does not name specific features. Scope creep prevention requires explicit item enumeration with reasoning.

antipattern.description: Writing spec.md after design.md is already complete.
antipattern.reason: Back-filled requirements are never used for design decisions; traceability is fictional, not functional.
Feature scope is too broad. Split into sub-features; large specs fragment agent context and human review.
Violates 60% Must cap. Forces MVP scope inflation. Re-classify ruthlessly.

text.reference: Full spec.md template with all sections
Agent prompt for authoring spec from brief
Agent prompt for FR-US-AC traceability review
